structural_parity.description: Structural parity validation: verify generated tasks have required files (task.toml, instruction.md, Dockerfile, test.sh, verify.py, test_patch.diff, task_metadata.json) and valid metadata.

checks_per_task: task_metadata.json valid JSON with required keys

structural_parity.notes: 3/3 tasks passed structural validation after bug fixes. Dockerfile now uses FROM hilbench-base:<task_id> (predictable tag, no build arg). Runner tags loaded images to match. Removed || true from apt-get install.

eval_parity.description: End-to-end evaluation parity: build Docker image, run test.sh, verify correct rewards.
eval_parity.notes: ScaleAI/hil-bench-swe-images is a HuggingFace bucket, not a dataset repo. Runner translates hf://buckets/ScaleAI/hil-bench-swe-images/images/<uid>.tar.zst to https://huggingface.co/buckets/ScaleAI/hil-bench-swe-images/resolve/images/<uid>.tar.zst before docker load. Local eval parity refreshed on public_swe_0: image downloaded, docker loaded, retagged to hilbench-base:public-swe-0, generated Dockerfile built, /workspace mapped to /app, verifier wrote reward 0.0 for unsolved task and reward 1.0 after applying the ground-truth patch. Local Docker host was linux/aarch64 and HILBench image was linux/amd64, so Docker emitted a platform warning but the build and verifier completed.
